claim: Anthropic's official Claude Code devcontainer Dockerfile uses node:20 as its base, installs Claude Code via npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code, runs as the non-root 'node' user, and keeps the tool set intentionally minimal (git, gh, jq, fzf, zsh, iptables for a firewall script).
quote: FROM node:20 ... apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends less git procps sudo fzf zsh man-db unzip gnupg2 gh iptables ipset iproute2 dnsutils aggregate jq nano vim ... RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@${CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION}

claim: HolyClaude uses node:22-bookworm-slim as its base and a single-stage build, creating a 'claude' user with passwordless sudo and installing Claude Code via the native installer at claude.ai/install.sh.
quote: FROM node:22-bookworm-slim ... RUN usermod -l claude -d /home/claude -m node && groupmod -n claude node && echo "claude ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" > /etc/sudoers.d/claude ... RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

claim: HolyClaude's apt layer installs the full media/doc stack — chromium, pandoc, ffmpeg, libvips-dev, imagemagick, tesseract-adjacent fonts-noto-core, plus ripgrep, fd-find, jq, bat, fzf, tmux, postgresql-client, redis-tools, sqlite3 — with --no-install-recommends and /var/lib/apt/lists cleanup.
quote: apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git curl wget jq ripgrep fd-find unzip zip tree tmux fzf bat bubblewrap build-essential pkg-config python3 python3-pip python3-venv chromium fonts-liberation2 ... xvfb imagemagick sudo ... if [ "$VARIANT" = "full" ]; then apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends pandoc ffmpeg libvips-dev

claim: HolyClaude's Python layer installs openpyxl, python-docx, python-pptx, Pillow, and playwright via `pip install --break-system-packages` directly against Debian's system Python 3, bypassing PEP 668.
quote: pip install --no-cache-dir --break-system-packages requests httpx beautifulsoup4 lxml Pillow pandas numpy openpyxl python-docx ... playwright apprise ... python-pptx

claim: Playwright's supported Docker path is `npx playwright install --with-deps chromium firefox webkit`, which downloads browser binaries plus every system lib (libnss3, libasound2, libatk-bridge2.0, libgbm, libxkbcommon, etc.); Firefox and WebKit are glibc-only, so Alpine/musl bases are unsupported.
quote: Browser builds for Firefox and WebKit are built for the glibc library. Alpine Linux and other distributions that are based on the musl standard library are not supported.

claim: Microsoft's reference Playwright Docker image is ubuntu:noble with Node.js 24 from NodeSource, and relies on `playwright-core install --with-deps` to bake in all three browsers plus their system deps under /ms-playwright — it does not enumerate apt packages by hand.

Debian 12 bookworm is officially supported by Playwright for Chromium, Firefox and WebKit on both x86_64 and arm64, making debian:bookworm-slim or node:22-bookworm-slim viable bases for a full-browser image.
quote: Playwright supports Debian 12 Bookworm on both x86_64 and arm64 for Chromium, Firefox and WebKit.

claim: For .NET SDK 9 in a non-.NET base image, Microsoft documents two clean paths: copy from `mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:9.0-bookworm-slim` in a multi-stage build, or run `curl -sSL https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh | bash /dev/stdin -Channel 9.0 -InstallDir /usr/share/dotnet` and symlink to /usr/bin/dotnet.

dotnet-install.sh requires bash plus ca-certificates, curl, libc6, libgcc-s1, libicu74, libssl3t64, libstdc++6 and tzdata on Debian/Ubuntu; these must be installed before running the script because dotnet-install does not pull system dependencies itself.
quote: apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates curl libc6 libgcc-s1 libicu74 libssl3t64 libstdc++6 tzdata

claim: The Astral-recommended uv install pattern in Docker is to copy the static binary from the distroless image — `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.11.7 /uv /uvx /bin/` — which avoids needing curl+certs in the base and is reproducible when pinned to a tag or digest.

nvm inside Docker is a known PATH trap: each RUN opens a fresh non-interactive, non-login shell that does not source ~/.bashrc, so nvm's PATH munging is lost — fixes are to chain install+use in one RUN with `. $NVM_DIR/nvm.sh`, set SHELL to a login bash, or export NODE_PATH/PATH via ENV after install.
quote: PATH wrong (node not found) when running container-image with non-interactive shell like in Gitlab CICD-Jobs

claim: The community 'claude-office-skills' toolbelt nails the doc-conversion trio: LibreOffice's `soffice` for PPTX->PDF, Poppler's `pdftoppm` for PDF->image rasterization, and Pandoc for DOCX text extraction preserving tracked changes — paired with python-pptx, openpyxl, pypdf, Pillow, pdf2image and markitdown on the Python side.
quote: LibreOffice: soffice for PPTX to PDF conversion. Poppler: pdftoppm for PDF to image conversion. Pandoc: Document text extraction with tracked changes.

claim: Claude Code's Read tool silently degrades on PDFs when poppler-utils is missing — a common gotcha in node:22-bookworm based containers where it is not pre-installed, so poppler-utils should be an explicit apt line in any YOLO Dockerfile.
quote: Read tool cannot find poppler-utils in headless (-p) mode

claim: HolyClaude's 'full' image lands at roughly 4 GB and the 'slim' variant around 2 GB — a realistic size budget for a YOLO container once chromium, Playwright browsers, LibreOffice/Pandoc/ffmpeg and a full Python+Node stack are all in one layer-cake.
quote: Bundles Chromium, Playwright, and a full dev toolchain in the full image (~4GB); slim variant (~2GB) without browser components.

claim: Anthropic's devcontainer layout splits concerns into devcontainer.json (VS Code/Codespaces settings and mounts), Dockerfile (image + tools), and init-firewall.sh (iptables egress allow-list) — a three-file pattern worth copying for any YOLO sandbox that wants persisted auth and restricted network.
quote: The devcontainer setup consists of three primary components: devcontainer.json, Dockerfile, and init-firewall.sh (which establishes network security rules).
